html:
[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嗜酒 - Common phrase meaning 'to love drinking' or 'to be fond of alcohol.'
  • 感其身世微贱 - Combines '感' (to feel) with '身世' (one's life circumstances) and '微贱' (lowly or humble), expressing a sense of being moved by one's lowly status.
  • 何日脱此厄 - Rhetorical question meaning 'when will I escape this misfortune?' or 'when will I be free from this hardship?'
  • 如梦非梦 - Could mean 'like a dream but not quite a dream' or 'in a dream-like state.'
  • 何叹也 - Rhetorical question meaning 'why are you sighing?' or 'what are you lamenting?'
  • 薄暮 - Means 'late afternoon' or 'dusk.'
  • 耳语 - Means 'whispering' or 'speaking in a low voice.'
  • 亟 - Means 'hastily' or 'urgently.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邵武威果卒陈升 - Refers to a soldier named Chen Sheng from the Shao Wu Wei Guo regiment.
  • 绍兴十三年 - Refers to the 13th year of the Shaoxing era (1143 AD) during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 谯门 - Refers to the watchtower gate of a city or fortress.
  • 太守 - Refers to the governor of a prefecture.
  • 承信郎 - A rank in the Song Dynasty's military hierarchy.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈升 - The main character, a soldier.
  • 邵武威果卒 - The regiment to which Chen Sheng belongs.
  • 军校 - Military officers or commanders.
  • 谯门 - The watchtower gate of a city or fortress.
  • 太守 - The governor of a prefecture.
  • 承信郎 - A rank in the Song Dynasty's military hierarchy.

[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 了达 - Buddhist term referring to a monk named 'Lieda.'
  • 行脚 - Buddhist term meaning 'wandering' or 'traveling' for spiritual practice.
  • 治装 - Literally 'to prepare clothes,' used here to mean 'to pack.'
  • 碎绢纸 - Refers to small pieces of silk and paper.
  • 新生鼠未开目者五枚 - Literally 'five newborn mice whose eyes have not opened.'
  • 啾啾然 - Onomatopoeic expression describing the sound of the mice.
  • 托以他故不往 - Literally 'excused with another reason and did not go.'

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鱼顾子 - Literal descriptive phrase meaning 'the fish looked back at its offspring'; used to describe a specific behavior of fish during the breeding season.
  • 出行部 - Means 'to go out on a tour of inspection,' indicating an official visit.
  • 命庖人鲙之 - Means 'ordered the cook to slice it (the fish) thinly,' referring to the preparation of fish for cooking.
  • 方操刀间 - Means 'just as he was holding the knife,' describing the moment before the fish was sliced.
  • 方春时 - Means 'during the spring season,' setting the time frame for the events.
  • 产子苇间 - Means 'give birth to offspring in the reeds,' describing the fish's breeding behavior.
  • 其母日往来顾之 - Means 'the mother comes back and forth to look after them,' indicating the mother's care for her young.
  • 至成鱼乃去 - Means 'until they grow into fish and then leave,' describing the developmental process of the fish.
  • 或母获则子不能育 - Means 'if the mother is caught, the offspring cannot survive,' highlighting the dependency of the young on their mother.
  • 渔者以是候之云 - Means 'fishermen use this behavior to catch them,' explaining the practical application of the fish's behavior.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 井度 - A historical figure, possibly an official or a person of importance during the period.
  • 成都漕 - Refers to a position or role related to the management of waterways or transportation in Chengdu.
  • 蜀州新津 - A specific location in Sichuan Province, indicating where the events took place.
  • 夷坚甲志 - A collection of stories and anecdotes, likely a literary work or historical record.
  • 杜莘老起莘说 - A reference to a historical account or anecdote recorded by Du Xunle or Du Xun, suggesting that this story is part of a larger collection of tales.

Proper Nouns

  • 井度 - A historical figure, possibly an official or a person of importance during the period.
  • 成都漕 - A position or role related to the management of waterways or transportation in Chengdu.
  • 蜀州新津 - A specific location in Sichuan Province.
  • 夷坚甲志 - A collection of stories and anecdotes, likely a literary work or historical record.
  • 杜莘老起莘说 - A reference to a historical account or anecdote recorded by Du Xunle or Du Xun.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 开源宫主 - A term referring to a spiritual or divine position, likely a title given to a deity or a high-ranking spiritual figure.
  • 致仕 - Literally means 'to retire from office,' but in this context, it refers to the act of resigning from a government position.
  • 肆赦 - Means 'to issue a general pardon,' often associated with significant events or festivals.
  • 告老 - A polite way of saying 'to retire due to old age.'
  • 丁母忧 - Refers to the period of mourning observed by a son after the death of his mother, during which he is expected to refrain from official duties.
  • 感疾 - Means 'to contract an illness,' often implying that the illness was caused by emotional distress.
  • 正昼忽起 - Describes a sudden action taken during the day, specifically in the middle of the day.
  • 书奎文二字 - Means 'to write the characters 'Kuiwen,' which could be a reference to a specific text or title.
  • 具谢表 - Refers to the formal act of preparing a memorial or petition to express gratitude or resignation.
  • 整衣起坐 - Describes the act of getting up and sitting properly, often used to indicate a person's final moments or preparations for death.
  • 从治命 - Means 'to follow the will or command,' often in the context of fulfilling a final wish.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刘允、字厚中 - Duliù, a historical figure, whose name and courtesy name are provided.
  • 潮州海阳人 - Indicates that Duliù was from Haifeng, a district in Chaozhou, Guangdong province.
  • 绍兴四年进士第 - Refers to the year 1134 AD, when Duliù passed the imperial examination and became a Jinshi (advanced degree holder).
  • 宣和甲辰 - Refers to the year 1124 AD, using the Chinese sexagenary cycle.
  • 循州 - An ancient administrative division, roughly corresponding to modern-day Huizhou in Guangdong.
  • 奎文殿学士 - A high-ranking scholar-official position, often associated with the imperial library or a prestigious academic institution.
  • 开源宫主 - Likely a reference to a spiritual or divine position, possibly related to Daoist or Buddhist traditions.
  • 闾里 - Refers to the local community or neighborhood, often used in classical Chinese to describe the social environment.
  • 饮食起居 - Refers to daily activities such as eating, drinking, and living, often used to describe a person's lifestyle.
  • 亲旧交口劝勉 - Describes the act of close friends and family members encouraging someone to resume their duties.
  • 丧葬过度 - Refers to excessive mourning or funeral practices, often criticized in classical texts for being wasteful or inappropriate.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘允 - The historical figure being described.
  • 潮州海阳 - The place of origin of Duliù.
  • 绍兴四年 - The year 1134 AD.
  • 宣和甲辰 - The year 1124 AD.
  • 循州 - An ancient administrative division.
  • 奎文殿学士 - A high-ranking scholar-official position.
  • 开源宫主 - A spiritual or divine position.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 四支皆可伸屈 - Literal description meaning 'all four limbs can stretch and bend,' used to describe a person's physical flexibility.
  • 時方烝暑 - Classical expression meaning 'the weather is hot and humid,' indicating a specific climatic condition.
  • 色不少变 - Means 'the color (or appearance) does not change much,' suggesting a lack of visible discomfort despite the heat.
  • 刘少时 - Refers to 'when Liu was young,' indicating a time in Liu's life.
  • 当元佑丙寅中秋之夕 - Specifies a particular date: 'on the Mid-Autumn night of the year Bingyin during the Yuan佑 era.'
  • 梦游一洞府 - Means 'dreamed of visiting a cave temple,' indicating a dream experience.
  • 见塑像道装 - Describes seeing a statue dressed in Daoist attire.
  • 青娥在旁指曰 - Means 'a young woman beside him pointed and said,' indicating a person pointing out something.
  • 此公前身也 - Means 'this is his former incarnation,' suggesting a belief in reincarnation.
  • 既寤作八诗以纪之 - Means 'after waking up, he wrote eight poems to record it,' indicating a creative response to the dream.
  • 至是颇应云 - Means 'by this point, it was quite appropriate to say,' suggesting a fitting conclusion or observation.
  • 其诗曰 - Introduces the following lines as poetry.
  • 银筑层台玉砌成 - Poetic description of a silver-built terrace and jade-paved floor.
  • 五云深映百花明 - Describes five clouds deeply reflecting among myriad flowers.
  • 兽环响彻重门启 - Means 'the sound of a beast ring echoes through the heavy doors being opened.'
  • 无限青娥喜笑迎 - Means 'an infinite number of young women welcome with joyful laughter.'
  • 一青鬟前引度回廓 - Means 'a girl with a bun leads the way around the inner hall.'
  • 帘卷云间旧院堂 - Describes a curtain rolling up to reveal an old courtyard hall among clouds.
  • 松桂满庭龟鹤在 - Means 'pine and cassia trees fill the courtyard, with turtles and cranes present.'
  • 俨然丰观道家装 - Means 'clearly dressed in the attire of a Daoist master.'
  • 徐入东堂百步余 - Means 'gradually entering the eastern hall, over a hundred steps.'
  • 虚堂犹记旧来居 - Means 'the empty hall still remembers the old residence.'
  • 牕纱掩映琼签轴 - Describes window screens partially covering exquisite scrolls.
  • 尽是当时读遍书 - Means 'all were books read thoroughly at that time.'
  • 曈昽瑞日照觚棱 - Describes the gentle rays of the sun illuminating the corners of a building.
  • 溶曳祥烟远栋甍 - Means 'the smoke of good fortune flows and trails far into the roof beams.'
  • 松桧雅知人趣尚 - Means 'pine and cypress trees understand human tastes and preferences.'
  • 风来偏作步虚声 - Means 'the wind comes and makes a sound like the *Buxu* chant.'
  • 侧金坛畔虬松老 - Describes an old twisted pine tree beside a golden altar.
  • 甃玉池边绶鹝长 - Means 'a long ribbon-like plant grows by a jade-paved pool.'
  • 吟折紫芝香满手 - Means 'chanting while picking purple ginseng, the fragrance fills the hand.'
  • 数声鸣凤在修篁 - Means 'several calls of a phoenix in tall bamboo groves.'
  • 兽炉烟和百花香 - Describes the smoke from a beast-shaped incense burner mingling with the fragrance of a hundred flowers.
  • 玉叶琼枝倚两旁 - Means 'jade leaves and jade branches lean against both sides.'
  • 一曲云和鸾鹤舞 - Means 'a melody played on the cloud harp, with phoenixes and cranes dancing.'
  • 劝人争捧九霞觞 - Means 'encouraging people to vie for the cups of nine-colored clouds.'
  • 云母屏间看旧题 - Means 'looking at old inscriptions between cloud-mother screens.'
  • 醉吟阿母碧桃枝 - Means 'drunkenly chanting under the green peach branches of the Mother.'
  • 群仙指点未题处 - Means 'the group of immortals point to places not yet inscribed.'
  • 更乞凌虚白鹤词 - Means 'further asking for a poem about a white crane soaring in the void.'
  • 步出朱宫日渐移 - Means 'stepping out of the red palace as the sun gradually moves.'
  • 青鬟罗拜问归期 - Means 'the girls in buns prostrate themselves and ask about the return date.'
  • 尘缘若断人间世 - Means 'if the worldly ties are severed, it's like breaking off from the human world.'
  • 看取蟠桃正熟时 - Means 'look at the peaches of immortality when they are ripe.'
  • 潮人陈安国尝叙其事 - Means 'Chen Anguo from Chaoyang once narrated this story.'
  • 昉后更名旦 - Means 'after this, he changed his name to Dan.'
  • 仕至太常少卿 - Means 'he served up to the position of Junior Minister of Rites.'
  • 绍兴庚午 - Refers to the year Gengwu during the Shaoxing era.
  • 终于直龙图阁知潭州 - Means 'he died as the Director of the Dragon-Graph Pavilion and Governor of Tanzhou.'
  • 景尝知台州 - Means 'Jing once served as the governor of Taizhou.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 四支皆可伸屈 - Describes a physical state, possibly referring to a person with extraordinary flexibility.
  • 时方烝暑 - Indicates a specific season, likely summer.
  • 色不少变 - Suggests a person's composure or resilience.
  • 刘少时 - Refers to a specific period in Liu's life.
  • 当元佑丙寅中秋之夕 - A precise date, indicating a significant event in Liu's life.
  • 梦游一洞府 - A common theme in Chinese literature, often associated with spiritual or mystical experiences.
  • 见塑像道装 - Refers to Daoist imagery, which is prevalent in Chinese culture.
  • 青娥在旁指曰 - A young woman, often a symbol of beauty and grace.
  • 此公前身也 - Belief in reincarnation, a concept found in various religious traditions.
  • 既寤作八诗以纪之 - Writing poetry as a form of recording and preserving experiences.
  • 至是颇应云 - A concluding remark, often used to summarize or reflect on the narrative.
  • 其诗曰 - Introduces the following lines as poetry, which is a significant part of Chinese literary tradition.
  • 银筑层台玉砌成 - Poetic imagery of luxury and refinement.
  • 五云深映百花明 - Symbolic imagery of beauty and harmony.
  • 兽环响彻重门启 - Describes a grand and ceremonial setting.
  • 无限青娥喜笑迎 - Imagery of a welcoming and joyful atmosphere.
  • 一青鬟前引度回廓 - A detailed description of a scene in the dream.
  • 帘卷云间旧院堂 - Imagery of a mystical and ethereal setting.
  • 松桂满庭龟鹤在 - Symbolic of longevity and wisdom.
  • 俨然丰观道家装 - Describes a person dressed in traditional Daoist attire.
  • 徐入东堂百步余 - A gradual and deliberate movement, often used to build suspense.
  • 虚堂犹记旧来居 - A sense of nostalgia and memory.
  • 牕纱掩映琼签轴 - Imagery of a refined and cultured environment.
  • 尽是当时读遍书 - Indicates a well-read and knowledgeable person.
  • 曈昽瑞日照觚棱 - Describes a peaceful and serene morning.
  • 溶曳祥烟远栋甍 - Symbolic of good fortune and prosperity.
  • 松桧雅知人趣尚 - Implies a deep understanding of human nature.
  • 风来偏作步虚声 - A mystical and spiritual sound.
  • 侧金坛畔虬松老 - Describes an ancient and revered place.
  • 甃玉池边绶鹝长 - Imagery of a beautiful and tranquil setting.
  • 吟折紫芝香满手 - A poetic and spiritual act.
  • 数声鸣凤在修篁 - Symbolic of purity and nobility.
  • 兽炉烟和百花香 - Describes a harmonious and fragrant environment.
  • 玉叶琼枝倚两旁 - Imagery of beauty and elegance.
  • 一曲云和鸾鹤舞 - A scene of celestial beings dancing.
  • 劝人争捧九霞觞 - Encouraging people to vie for the cups of nine-colored clouds.
  • 云母屏间看旧题 - Looking at old inscriptions between cloud-mother screens.
  • 醉吟阿母碧桃枝 - Drunkenly chanting under the green peach branches of the Mother.
  • 群仙指点未题处 - The group of immortals point to places not yet inscribed.
  • 更乞凌虚白鹤词 - Further asking for a poem about a white crane soaring in the void.
  • 步出朱宫日渐移 - Stepping out of the red palace as the sun gradually moves.
  • 青鬟罗拜问归期 - The girls in buns prostrate themselves and ask about the return date.
  • 尘缘若断人间世 - If the worldly ties are severed, it's like breaking off from the human world.
  • 看取蟠桃正熟时 - Look at the peaches of immortality when they are ripe.
  • 潮人陈安国尝叙其事 - Chen Anguo from Chaoyang once narrated this story.
  • 昉后更名旦 - After this, he changed his name to Dan.
  • 仕至太常少卿 - He served up to the position of Junior Minister of Rites.
  • 绍兴庚午 - Refers to the year Gengwu during the Shaoxing era.
  • 终于直龙图阁知潭州 - He died as the Director of the Dragon-Graph Pavilion and Governor of Tanzhou.
  • 景尝知台州 - Jing once served as the governor of Taizhou.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 漳民娶山鬼 - A resident of Zhangzhou marries a mountain ghost, referring to a supernatural or mythical creature.
  • 為漳州司理參軍 - A local government official in charge of judicial matters.
  • 負薪為業 - Literally 'carrying firewood,' referring to a manual laborer who gathers and sells firewood.
  • 久之得一婦人 - After a long time, he gets a woman.
  • 遂與歸 - Literally 'and brought her home,' indicating the act of taking the woman back to his residence.
  • 以二籠自隨 - Literally 'with two baskets following him,' suggesting he carried two baskets, possibly for firewood or other items.
  • 其家皆喜 - The family of the man who married the woman is happy.
  • 唯民妹獨見婦一足 - Only the man’s sister saw one foot of the woman, indicating something unusual about the woman’s appearance.
  • 不敢言 - Dared not speak, suggesting the sister was hesitant to express her concerns.
  • 至夜同寢 - Until night, they slept together after dark.
  • 日高不啟門 - The sun was high, indicating that a significant amount of time had passed, and they did not open the door.
  • 父母壞壁以入 - The parents broke the wall to enter the room.
  • 但白骨在床 - Only white bones were found on the bed.
  • 皆瓦石及紙錢耳 - Only pottery shards, stones, and paper money were found.
  • 蓋山魈類也 - Inferred from the description of the skeletal remains and the contents of the chest, referring to a mythical creature.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 建州人范周翰 - A local official from Jianzhou, a region in Fujian.
  • 漳州司理參軍 - A local government official responsible for judicial matters, reflecting the administrative structure of the time.
  • 山魈 - A mythical creature in Chinese folklore, often associated with mountains and considered a malevolent spirit.
  • 崇寧五年 - The year 1106, providing a specific historical context for the story.
  • 朝奉待 - A title for a low-ranking official in the Song dynasty.
  • 齊述叛 - Refers to a rebel leader during the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 陰府 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead in Chinese mythology.
  • 三木 - Refers to three wooden restraints, often used as a form of punishment or binding in Chinese folklore.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '漳民娶山鬼' is elliptical and relies on the reader’s familiarity with the context to understand that the man married a supernatural being.
  • The phrase '其家皆喜' is ambiguous in terms of who exactly is included in '其家' (the family). It could refer to the man’s family or the woman’s family.
  • The sentence '唯民妹獨見婦一足' is compact, with the sister’s observation placed in a subordinate clause.
  • The sentence '父母壞壁以入' is elliptical, with the action of breaking the wall and entering the room implied.
  • The conclusion '蓋山魈類也' is inferred from the previous description of the skeletal remains and the contents of the chest.

Proper Nouns

  • 漳民 - Refers to a resident of Zhangzhou.
  • 范周翰 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 建州 - A region in Fujian.
  • 漳州 - A region in Fujian.
  • 齊述 - The name of a rebel leader.
  • 崇寧 - The era name of Emperor Huizong of Song.
  • 朝奉待 - A title for a low-ranking official.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建炎末 - End of the Jianyan era (1127–1130) during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 知桂阳监 - '知' means 'to govern' or 'to serve as an official in charge of'; '桂阳监' refers to the administrative office in Guiyang, Hunan.
  • 湖湘多盗 - '湖湘' refers to the regions of Hunan and Hubei, where banditry was common.
  • 一切绳以重法 - '一切' means 'all' or 'every'; '绳以重法' means to enforce strict laws.
  • 入狱者多死 - '入狱者' refers to prisoners; '多死' means 'mostly died'.
  • 绕项皆生痈疽 - '绕项' means 'around the neck'; '痈疽' refers to a severe infection or abscess.
  • 喉管皆见 - '喉管' means 'throat'; '皆见' means 'all visible'.
  • 如受斩刑者 - '如受斩刑' means 'like someone who has been beheaded'.
  • 命家人作烝鸭欲食 - '命家人' means 'ordered the family members'; '作烝鸭' means 'to prepare steamed duck'; '欲食' means 'wanted to eat'.
  • 司理院推吏忽自语 - '司理院' refers to the judicial office; '推吏' means 'officials in charge of investigations'; '忽自语' means 'suddenly spoke to themselves'.
  • 官追我证吴知郡公事 - '官追我' means 'officials are pursuing me'; '证吴知郡公事' means 'to testify about the affairs of the governor of Guiyang'.
  • 曹绅以宣义郎摄机宜 - '曹绅' is the name of a person; '宣义郎' is an official title; '摄机宜' means 'to temporarily serve as an advisor'.
  • 连公前后所杀海寇不可计 - '连公' refers to Lian Nanfu; '前后所杀海寇' means 'the pirates he killed before and after'; '不可计' means 'uncountable'.
  • 曹皆手处其事 - '曹皆手处其事' means 'Cao handled everything personally'.
  • 迁官至朝奉大夫 - '迁官' means 'promoted to a higher rank'; '朝奉大夫' is an official title.
  • 广倅 - '广' refers to Guangzhou; '倅' means 'deputy governor'.
  • 公宇在净慧寺 - '公宇' refers to the official's residence; '净慧寺' is the name of a temple.
  • 一妾生子 - '一妾' means 'a concubine'; '生子' means 'gave birth to a child'.
  • 遍体皆长毛 - '遍体' means 'all over the body'; '长毛' means 'covered in hair'.
  • 瘗之山下 - '瘗' means 'buried'; '山下' means 'at the foot of the mountain'.
  • 经三日发视 - '经三日' means 'after three days'; '发视' means 'to open and see'.
  • 盖冤鬼所托云 - '盖' means 'probably'; '冤鬼' means 'vindictive spirits'; '所托云' means 'said to be the result of'.
  • 五事皆张可久说 - '五事' means 'five events'; '皆' means 'all'; '张可久' is the name of the person who told these stories.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 建炎 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song, referring to the early years of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 桂阳监 - An administrative office in Guiyang, Hunan, during the Song Dynasty.
  • 湖湘 - The regions of Hunan and Hubei, known for their mountainous terrain and historical significance.
  • 宣义郎 - An official title in the Song Dynasty, indicating a certain rank in the bureaucracy.
  • 朝奉大夫 - A higher-ranking official title in the Song Dynasty.
  • 广倅 - The deputy governor of Guangzhou, a significant administrative position.
  • 净慧寺 - A temple in Guangzhou, likely an important religious site during the Song Dynasty.
  • 冤鬼 - Vindictive spirits believed to seek revenge on those who wronged them.
  • 张可久 - A historical figure who recounted these stories, possibly a scholar or historian.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴仲弓 - The name of the governor of Guiyang.
  • 建炎 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 桂阳监 - The administrative office in Guiyang, Hunan.
  • 湖湘 - The regions of Hunan and Hubei.
  • 宣义郎 - An official title in the Song Dynasty.
  • 朝奉大夫 - A higher-ranking official title in the Song Dynasty.
  • 广倅 - The deputy governor of Guangzhou.
  • 净慧寺 - A temple in Guangzhou.
  • 曹绅 - The name of a person who served as an advisor.
  • 连南夫 - The name of a person, likely a military leader.
  • 张可久 - The name of the person who recounted these stories.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乡人 - Villager or local resident.
  • 偕诣 - Together went to.
  • 拱 - To offer or present.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '建炎二年，邓民残于胡兵，或俘或死，晁氏男女数百人，皆囚以北，至汾州青灰山，为红巾邵伯邀击，尽失所掠而去' describes a series of events leading to the capture and subsequent loss of the Zhao family members.
  • The sequence '张丞相宣抚陕蜀，邵举军来降，王生为右军小将，与晁妇同处于阆中' describes the political and military context following the events in Deng.
  • The sequence '阆有灵显王庙，妇与乳妪以月二日往焚香，妪视道上一丐者，病以敝纸自蔽，形容甚悴，谛观之，以告妇，曰、有丐者绝类吾十一郎，遣询其乡里姓行' describes a specific incident involving the Zhao wife and her wet nurse.

  • 宜春郡官 - An official in Yichun Prefecture.

[403]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 向得其发言颇喜 - When I heard his words, I was very pleased.
  • 顾小史令持往所馆问之 - I then asked the young attendant to take it to our lodging and ask him.
  • 此常物尔 - This is just an ordinary thing.
  • 何遽为 - Why are you so hasty?
  • 将置吾棺中 - I will put it in my coffin.
  • 向始疑惧 - I began to feel doubtful and fearful.
  • 引其手使少憩 - I held his hand and made him rest a bit.
  • 亦不动 - He also did not move.
  • 亟招郑君同视之 - I quickly called V

[404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 願君力疾告我 - Polite expression meaning 'I hope you can summon your strength to tell me,' indicating a request for information despite the speaker's illness.
  • 所以不欲來及危惙如此之状 - Complex phrase meaning 'the reason why you do not wish to come and the state of your illness being so severe,' combining the cause and the condition.
  • 黃開目傾聽 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Huang opened his eyes to listen,' emphasizing Huang's attentive state.
  • 忍痛言曰 - Means 'enduring pain, he said,' highlighting the speaker's suffering while speaking.
  • 吾官於此時 - Means 'I was serving in this position,' referring to the speaker's role at the time.
  • 宜春尉遣弓手三人 - Means 'the magistrate of Yichun sent three archers,' indicating the official's action.
  • 買雞豚於村墅 - Means 'to buy chickens and pigs in the countryside,' describing the task given to the archers.
  • 閱四十日不歸 - Means 'after forty days, they did not return,' indicating the duration of their absence.
  • 三人之妻訴於郡 - Means 'the wives of the three men appealed to the prefecture,' showing the wives' action.
  • 郡守與尉有舊好 - Means 'the prefecture governor had old friendships with the magistrate,' explaining the relationship between the officials.
  • 令尉自為計 - Means 'he ordered the magistrate to handle it himself,' indicating the governor's instruction.
  • 尉绐白府曰 - Means 'the magistrate deceived the prefecture and said,' showing the magistrate's deceptive action.
  • 部內有盜起 - Means 'there was a bandit uprising in the area,' describing the situation.
  • 已得其根株窟穴所在 - Means 'we have already found the roots and lairs of the bandits,' indicating the progress made.
  • 遣三人者往偵 - Means 'we sent the three men to investigate,' describing the action taken.
  • 恐其徒泄此謀 - Means 'fearing that their accomplices would reveal this plan,' explaining the concern.
  • 姑以買物為名 - Means 'we used the excuse of buying goods,' indicating the cover story.
  • 是殆斃於賊手 - Means 'they are likely to be killed by the bandits,' expressing the fear.
  • 願合諸邑求盜 - Means 'I hope to combine the efforts of all counties to catch the bandits,' showing the speaker's request.
  • 吏卒共捕之 - Means 'officials and soldiers will capture them together,' describing the joint action.
  • 守然其言 - Means 'the governor agreed with his words,' indicating the governor's response.
  • 尉自將以往 - Means 'the magistrate led them himself,' showing the magistrate's action.
  • 留山間兩月 - Means 'they stayed in the mountains for two months,' indicating the duration of their stay.
  • 無以復命 - Means 'they could not report back,' showing the outcome of their mission.
  • 適村民四輩耕於野 - Means 'just then, four villagers were farming in the fields,' describing the encounter.
  • 貌蠢甚 - Means 'they looked very stupid,' indicating the appearance of the villagers.
  • 使從吏持錢二萬 - Means 'he sent a subordinate with 20,000 coins,' describing the action taken.
  • 招之與語 - Means 'he called them over to speak,' showing the interaction.
  • 三弓手為盜所殺 - Means 'the three archers were killed by the bandits,' describing the tragic outcome.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宜春尉 - Refers to the magistrate of Yichun, a county in Jiangxi Province.
  • 郡守 - Refers to the prefecture governor, a high-ranking official.
  • 弓手 - Refers to archers, who were often used as local security forces.
  • 雞豚 - Refers to chickens and pigs, common livestock in ancient China.
  • 村墅 - Refers to the countryside or rural areas.
  • 舊好 - Refers to old friendships or acquaintances.
  • 盜 - Refers to bandits or robbers, common threats in ancient times.
  • 根株窟穴 - Refers to the roots and lairs of bandits, indicating their base of operations.
  • 諸邑 - Refers to various counties or districts.
  • 吏卒 - Refers to officials and soldiers, the local law enforcement.

Proper Nouns

  • 黃 - The name of the listener, likely a person of some importance.
  • 宜春尉 - The title of the magistrate of Yichun.
  • 郡守 - The title of the prefecture governor.
  • 弓手 - The title of the archers.
  • 雞豚 - Common livestock in ancient China.
  • 村墅 - The place where the archers were sent to buy goods.
  • 舊好 - The relationship between the officials.
  • 盜 - The bandits or robbers.
  • 根株窟穴 - The base of operations of the bandits.
  • 諸邑 - Various counties or districts.
  • 吏卒 - Officials and soldiers.

[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尉來逐捕 - Means 'the magistrate came to pursue and capture,' indicating an official action.
  • 倩汝四人诈为盗以应命 - A complex phrase meaning 'I ask you four to pretend to be bandits to fulfill the order.' '倩' is a polite way to request someone to do something.
  • 屏狱吏以情诘之 - Means 'I dismissed the jailers and questioned them privately.'
  • 皆曰不冤 - Means 'they all said they were not wronged.'
  • 摘语之曰 - Means 'I picked out words to say to them.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 尉 - Refers to a local magistrate or official responsible for law enforcement.
  • 盗 - Refers to bandits or thieves.
  • 处斩 - Refers to a death sentence by beheading.
  • 杖 - Refers to a form of corporal punishment involving beating with a stick.
  • 妻孥 - Refers to one's wife and children.
  • 有司 - Refers to government officials or authorities.
  • 如问汝杀人 - Refers to a specific question about committing murder.
  • 屏狱吏 - Refers to dismissing or removing jailers from the presence.
  • 摘语之 - Refers to carefully selecting words to speak.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 身首一分 - Literal meaning 'head and body separated,' referring to death or decapitation.
  • 不可复续 - Meaning 'cannot be restored,' indicating the finality of death.
  • 囚相顾泣下 - Meaning 'the prisoners looked at each other and wept,' describing a shared emotional response.
  • 初以为死且复生归 - Meaning 'initially thought that after death they would return to life,' expressing a mistaken belief.
  • 家得钱用 - Meaning 'to earn money for the family,' indicating a practical expectation.
  • 不知果死也 - Meaning 'did not know that death was real,' revealing the prisoners' ignorance.
  • 始具言其故 - Meaning 'then they fully explained the reason,' indicating a detailed account.
  • 吾大惊 - Meaning 'I was greatly shocked,' expressing the translator's reaction.
  • 悉挺其缚 - Meaning 'completely untied their bindings,' describing the action taken.
  • 尉已伺知之 - Meaning 'the military officer had already discovered this,' indicating prior knowledge.
  • 密白守曰 - Meaning 'secretly reported to the governor saying,' describing a confidential communication.
  • 狱掾受囚赂 - Meaning 'the prison official accepted bribes from the prisoners,' indicating corruption.
  • 导之上变 - Meaning 'led to a change in the situation,' suggesting manipulation.
  • 明日吾入府白事 - Meaning 'the next day I will go to the government office to report,' indicating a planned action.
  • 守盛怒 - Meaning 'the governor was extremely angry,' describing the governor's reaction.
  • 叱使下 - Meaning 'shouted at them to step down,' indicating a command.
  • 君治狱已竟 - Meaning 'you have completed the case handling,' referring to the completion of a legal process.
  • 上诸外台阅实矣 - Meaning 'it has been reviewed by the higher authorities,' indicating official verification.
  • 乃受贿赂 - Meaning 'and then accepted bribes,' describing the act of corruption.
  • 妄欲改变邪 - Meaning 'you want to change it recklessly?' expressing skepticism.
  • 既得其冤 - Meaning 'having obtained their innocence,' indicating a legal outcome.
  • 安敢不为辨 - Meaning 'how dare I not defend them?' expressing a commitment to justice.
  • 移狱于录曹 - Meaning 'transferred the case to the recording office,' describing a procedural step.
  • 又移于县 - Meaning 'then transferred it to the county,' indicating further transfer.
  • 不能决 - Meaning 'could not decide,' indicating indecision.
  • 法当复申宪台 - Meaning 'according to law, it should be submitted to the constitutional court,' indicating legal procedure.
  • 则置狱 - Meaning 'then the case would be reopened,' indicating a potential reversal.
  • 守曰 - Meaning 'the governor said,' introducing a direct quote.
  • 如是则一郡失入之罪众矣 - Meaning 'if so, then the governor of the entire prefecture would bear the blame for wrongful convictions,' indicating a serious consequence.
  • 安有已论决而复变者 - Meaning 'how could there be a case already decided and then changed?' expressing disbelief.
  • 悉取移狱辞焚之 - Meaning 'completely burned all the transferred case documents,' describing a drastic action.
  • 但以付理院 - Meaning 'but handed it over to the legal department,' indicating a final disposition.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狱掾 - Refers to a prison official responsible for case handling.
  • 尉 - Refers to a military officer, often involved in local administration.
  • 守 - Refers to a governor or local official with administrative and judicial authority.
  • 外台 - Refers to higher-level administrative bodies, often overseeing local officials.
  • 宪台 - Refers to the constitutional court or a high-level judicial body.
  • 录曹 - Refers to a department responsible for recording and archiving legal documents.
  • 县 - Refers to a county, a basic administrative unit in ancient China.
  • 理院 - Refers to a legal department or court responsible for handling cases.

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 使如初款 - If one were to follow the initial agreement
  • 引义固争 - To argue based on principle or righteousness
  • 累十数日不得直 - After several days of arguing without success
  • 谒告 - To resign or take leave
  • 郡守令司户尝摄邑者代吾事 - The county magistrate ordered the head of the household register, who had previously governed the county, to take over my duties
  • 临欲杀囚 - On the verge of executing the prisoner
  • 守复悔曰 - The magistrate then regretted and said
  • 若黄司理不书狱 - If Judge Huang does not sign the case record
  • 异时必讼我于朝矣 - In the future, he will surely sue me in court
  • 令同官相镌谕曰 - The magistrate instructed his colleagues to advise me
  • 囚必死 - The prisoner will definitely die
  • 君虽固执 - Even if you insist
  • 亦无益 - It will be of no use
  • 今强为书名于牍尾 - Now, you must sign your name at the end of the document
  • 人人知事出郡将 - Everyone will know that this is the decision of the county magistrate
  • 君何罪焉 - What crime have you committed?
  • 吾黾俯书押 - I reluctantly signed the document
  • 四人遂死 - The four prisoners were executed
  • 越二日 - Two days later
  • 黄衣人持梃押二县吏来追院中二吏 - A yellow-robed person with a stick came to chase the two clerks in the courtyard
  • 急取案 - Hurry and get the case file
  • 吏方云云 - The clerks were talking
  • 黄衣以梃击之 - The yellow-robed person struck them with the stick
  • 四吏俱入舍不出 - The four clerks all entered their rooms and did not come out
  • 吾自往视 - I went to check myself
  • 舍门元未启 - The room door was not open
  • 望其中案牍横陈 - I saw the case files scattered inside
  • 逡巡四吏皆暴卒 - After a while, the four clerks suddenly died
  • 又数日摄令死 - A few days later, the acting magistrate died
  • 尉用他赏改秩 - The county magistrate was promoted with another reward

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 司理 - Refers to a judge or official responsible for judicial matters
  • 县吏 - Refers to county clerks or officials
  • 黄衣人 - Refers to a person dressed in yellow, possibly a supernatural being or a significant figure
  • 梃 - Refers to a stick or club, often used in physical confrontations
  • 暴卒 - Refers to a sudden and unexpected death, often with a supernatural connotation

Proper Nouns

  • 黄司理 - Refers to Judge Huang, a specific judicial official
  • 县吏 - Refers to county clerks or officials
  • 黄衣人 - Refers to a person dressed in yellow, possibly a supernatural being or a significant figure
